title: fix: pass image_urls array to grok-imagine-video
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Grokking the full picture
gist.blurb: The Grok video model now correctly handles image inputs, accepting the full array of URLs instead of just one. Your moving pictures just got a lot smoother.
gist.summary: Fixed an issue in the Airforce model integration where image-to-video requests were sending a single string instead of the required array of image URLs.
gist.impact: Users generating video with the Grok model can now successfully use image inputs for animation without API errors.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a film editor, splicing together a strip of film frames (representing the array of images) to feed into a projector. The film strip glows with the signature lime green.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot splicing together a glowing lime green (#ecf874) film strip on a vintage editing table. A chunky robot with a monitor head watches the projection with excitement. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Pixel text: 'ACTION!'. Nostalgic but beautiful.
